forecast-period.text: A strong high pressure system over the Tasman Sea will maintain a ridge over SA. A cold front over the state will weaken and clear to the east early Sunday. A new high pressure system will develop in the Bight on Sunday, drifting east early next week to merge with the strong high in the Tasman Sea.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones.

Showers about the agricultural area during the morning, clearing to the east during the day. Possible early morning thunderstorms about central areas. Possible early fog about the west. Maximum temperatures below average, tending slightly above average in the southeast. Southwest to southeasterly winds, fresh at times near the coasts.

Showers and isolated thunderstorms about the west extending east to reach Adelaide and southern coasts late tonight. Northeast to northwesterly winds freshening ahead of a south to southwesterly change continuing over the west and south.

Morning fog patches about the agricultural areas. Partly cloudy about the coasts, mostly sunny inland. Maximum temperatures slightly below average. South to southeasterly winds with afternoon seabreezes.

Mostly sunny. Possible early morning fog patches about the agricultural area. Maximum temperatures above average about the agricultural area grading to slightly below average in the far north. Northeast winds tending southeasterly about coastal districts.

text.p: Mostly sunny. Maximum temperatures above average. East to northeasterly winds.

Cumulative rainfall totals to the end of Wednesday: Generally less than 2 mm across the southern pastorals, and eastern border districts, reaching 2 to 5 mm across western and central agricultural areas. Localised higher totals of 5 to 10 mm with thunderstorms overnight and early Sunday morning, most likely about Lower Eyre Peninsula, Kangaroo Island and southern Yorke Peninsula.
